messages.content: I'm ready to help with your coding tasks. What do you need help with?
& 'c:\Users\Krithik\Desktop\opencode stuff\rev_eng\.gitignore' summarise this file, why are gitignore files used generally

The .gitignore file in the 'rev_eng' directory contains 4 lines, which specify files or directories to be ignored by Git. 

The files or directories being ignored are:
- node_modules/ (a directory where dependencies are installed by npm or yarn)
- dist/ (a directory where compiled or built files are stored)
- *.exe (any executable files)
- .env (a file containing environment variables)

Gitignore files are used to prevent certain files or directories from being tracked by Git. This is useful for several reasons:
- It reduces the size of the Git repository by excluding large or unnecessary files.
- It prevents sensitive information, such as environment variables or API keys, from being committed to the repository.
- It helps to keep the repository clean and organized by excluding files that are generated automatically or are not relevant to the project.
- It improves performance by reducing the amount of data that needs to be transferred during Git operations.
Gitignore files are an important part of Git workflow, helping to keep your repository organized and secure. If you have any more questions or need help with anything else, feel free to ask!
